properties.name: Ranakpur Chaumukha Temple
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Jain Vishwa Bharati records

facts: One of India's five holiest Shvetambar Jain pilgrimages ('Pancha-Tirtha') — built 1439 under Mewar's Rana Kumbha, funded by the merchant Dharna Shah.
1,444 marble pillars — no two carved identically; the temple's four-faced (chaumukha) idol of Adinath faces all four cardinal directions.
Set in an Aravalli valley 96 km north of Udaipur; the surrounding forest is a protected wildlife sanctuary.

properties.name: Dilwara Temples, Mount Abu
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Jain Vishwa Bharati records

facts: The most exquisitely carved Jain temple complex in India — five white marble temples built 11th-13th c. on Mount Abu.
Vimal Vasahi (1031, dedicated to Adinath) and Luna Vasahi (1230, dedicated to Neminath) are the two masterpieces.
The carved marble ceilings are so thin they become translucent — an artistic feat impossible to replicate.
Non-Jain visitors are allowed but with strict dress and behaviour codes; photography banned inside.

properties.name: Nakoda Parshvanath Tirtha
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Nakoda Trust records

facts: Major Shvetambar pilgrimage centre for the Nakoda Bhairav — a fierce guardian deity attached to Parshvanath.
Located in a remote Barmer valley; the black-marble Bhairav idol is said to fulfil wishes when offered laddoos.
Nakoda's dharamshala complex accommodates 10,000+ pilgrims during peak festivals.

properties.name: Rikhabdev (Kesariyaji)
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Jain Vishwa Bharati records

facts: Uniquely worshipped by BOTH Digambar and Shvetambar Jains as well as Bhil tribals (as their 'Kesariyaji Baba') — India's most cross-community Jain site.
The black-stone Rishabhanath idol is anointed daily with saffron paste ('kesar') — hence 'Kesariyaji'.
Located on the Udaipur-Ahmedabad highway; hosts Rajasthan's largest Digambar-Shvetambar unified congregation each year.

properties.name: Osian Jain Temples
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + ASI Rajasthan Circle

facts: Rajasthan's oldest surviving Jain temple — the Mahavira Temple at Osian, built 8th c. under the Pratihara emperor Vatsaraja.
Also home to the Sachiya Mata temple (Hindu goddess) — Osian is Rajasthan's earliest Hindu-Jain integrated temple complex.
The Oswal Jain merchant community traces its origin to Osian — legend has the Sachiya Mata converting them to Jainism.

properties.name: Shri Mahavirji
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Digambar Jain Atishaya Kshetra records

facts: Major Digambar Jain 'Atishaya Kshetra' (miracle-site) — the Mahavira idol was reportedly discovered underground by a cowherd in 1509.
Rajasthan's largest Digambar Jain congregation site; the annual Mahaveerji Mela draws ~200,000 pilgrims in Chaitra (Mar-Apr).
The rath yatra (chariot procession) passes through the Gambhira river — a defining Jain-Karauli tradition.
